content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Lorraine Washington 
city Clerk, 
City of Watsonville 
P.O. Box 430 
Watsonville, Ca 95077 
Dear Ms. Washington: 
November 20, 1989 
Re: 
Combined Campaign Report 
Our File No. I-89-645 
This is to confirm telephone advice provided to your office 
on November 3, 1989, regarding the campaign disclosure provisions 
of the Political Reform Act (the "Act,,).l 
This letter constitutes 
written advice under Section 84205. 
section 84205 states: 
The Commission may by regulation or written advice permit 
candidates and committees to file campaign statements 
combining statements and reports required to be filed by this 
title. 
/ 
The City of watsonville's November 7, 1989, election has been 
moved to December 5, 1989 because~of the recent earthquake. 
This 
postponement of the election will necessitate the filing of an 
additional pre-election statement which will cover through the 
17th day before the December 5 election. 
You have advised me that 
some candidates and committees filed statements on the original 
deadline for filing a second pre-election statements in connection 
with the November 7 election, which was due on October 26, 1989. 
We are advising that you not assess penalties to anyone who did 
not file that statement or filed it late. 
Candidates and 
comm-ittees which have not yet filed that statement should combine 
their 2nd pre-election statement with the additional pre-election 
statement. 
Those candidates and committees which did file the 2nd 
pre-election statement due October 26 should also file the 
additional pre-election statement due November 23, 1989. 
1 
Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
Section 18000, et ~ 
All references to regUlations are to Title 
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322,5660 


Lorraine Washington 
File No. I-89-645 
Page 2 
Therefore, campaign disclosure statements should be filed on 
the following schedule: 
Schedule for filers who filed the 2nd Pre-Election statement due 
on October 26, 1989. 
~ 
of Statement 
1st Pre-Election 
2nd Pre-Election 
Additional Pre-
Election 
Semi-Annual 
Period Covered 
Jan. 122- sept. 23 
sept. 24 - Oct. 21 
Oct. 22 -
Nov. 18 
Nov. 19 -
Dec. 31 
Filing Deadline 
september 24, 1989 
October 26, 1989 
November 23, 1989 
January 31, 1990 
Schedule for filers who did not file the 2nd Pre-Election 
statement due on October 26, 1989. 
~ 
of Statement 
1st Pre-Election 
2nd Pre-Election and 
Additional Pre-
Election 
Semi-Annual 
Period Covered 
Jan. 12 - Sept. 23 
Sept. 24 -
Nov. 18 
Nov. 19 -
Dec. 31 
/ 
Filing Deadline 
September 24, 1989 
November 23, 1989 
January 31, 1990 
If you have any questions regarding the above, please do not 
hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5662. 
.. 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel 
(;J 
~{~. 
By: ~. 
Imberi 
Political Reform Consultant 
The period covered by any statement begins on the day after 
the closing date of the last statement filed or January 1 if no 
previous statement has been filed. 
.' 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Lorraine washington 
City Clerk, 
City of watsonville 
P.o. Box 430 
Watsonville, CA 95077 
Dear Mso Washington: 
November 29, 1989 
Re: 
Our File No. 1-89-645 
This is to confirm telephone advice provided to a member of 
your staff on November 27, 1989 regarding my letter dated November 
20, 1989. 
The letter contains a typographical error in the 
section headed "Schedule for filers who filed the 2nd Pre-Election 
statement due on October 26, 1989." 
The letter incorrectly 
indicates that the period covered by the 1st Pre-Election 
statement is January 12 - September 23. 
It should have indicated 
that the period covered is January 1 -
September 23. 
Please 
accept our apologies for any problems or inconvenience this error 
may have caused. 
If you have any questions regarding the above, please do not 
hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5662. 
~,incerely, \~ 
. J I r;: \\ / 
vV r;t'~ 
. ~v(j-<"'/'A 
Wayn 
P. Imberi 
Political Reform Consultant 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.question_synthetic: Due to an earthquake-related postponement of Watsonville's election from November 7 to December 5, 1989, what campaign disclosure filing requirements and schedules apply to candidates and committees?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that no penalties be assessed for missing or late-filed second pre-election statements originally due October 26, 1989. Candidates and committees should follow specific filing schedules based on whether they filed the original statement, with the option to combine statements as permitted under Section 84205.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document consists of two related letters dated November 20 and November 29, 1989. The second letter corrects a typographical error in the first. No explicit QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections, but advice is clearly stated. Two different filing schedules are provided as tables.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Due to an earthquake-related postponement of Watsonville's election from November 7 to December 5, 1989, what campaign disclosure filing requirements and schedules apply to candidates and committees?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC advises that no penalties be assessed for missing or late-filed second pre-election statements originally due October 26, 1989. Candidates and committees should follow specific filing schedules based on whether they filed the original statement, with the option to combine statements as permitted under Section 84205.

embedding.summary: This FPPC advice letter (with subsequent correction) addresses campaign disclosure filing requirements for Watsonville's earthquake-postponed election, providing two filing schedules and guidance on combined statement filings and penalty waiver.
